title: Phlegmariurus bradeorum
taxonomy: Phlegmariurus bradeorum is a species of plant in the family Lycopodiaceae, commonly known as club mosses. This plant is a member of the genus Phlegmariurus, which is a group of evergreen, epiphytic or terrestrial plants. Phlegmariurus bradeorum is native to South America and is a part of the larger class Lycopodiopsida, which includes all club mosses and related species. The plant is further classified as a member of the order Hymnales, which is a group of vascular plants that reproduce via spores. At the kingdom level, Phlegmariurus bradeorum belongs to the kingdom Plantae, which includes all plant species.
uses: Phlegmariurus bradeorum is a species of plant that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. It is used in traditional medicine in certain regions to treat various health issues, including fever and pain. The plant is believed to contain bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Research on Phlegmariurus bradeorum is ongoing to further understand its medicinal applications and potential benefits to human health. Further studies are necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety for use in medicine.

preparations: Phlegmariurus bradeorum is a species of plant that has been used in traditional medicine in Brazil.
